steps.title: Locate the prior idea
steps.content: The sentence before the underlined one says Lily can be sensitive to a scene that fits the background of her own sensations. That introduces the idea that a landscape can reflect her inner mood.

steps.title: Read the underlined example
steps.content: The underlined sentence shows that idea in action: Lily sees the landscape as an enlargement of her present mood and finds something of herself in its calmness and breadth.

steps.title: Reject wrong function claims
steps.content:
• Choice A: The detailed physical imagery appears mainly in the following sentence.
  • Choice B: The sentence suggests identification and calmness, not internal conflict.
  • Choice C: The next sentence adds scenery details rather than expanding the mood claim.
So the underlined sentence illustrates the idea introduced just before it. The answer is D.
